tasks.task_name: Scaffold local Go and React application runtime
tasks.goal: The repository contains a Go 1.26.x backend and Vite React frontend that can run locally with documented developer commands.

inputs.name: spec
inputs.constraints: Includes FR-001 through FR-005 and UI technology requirements

outputs.name: runtime-source
outputs.constraints: Go module uses Go 1.26.x and backend serves /api/* plus embedded frontend assets

outputs.name: developer-commands
outputs.constraints: Commands cover backend tests, frontend tests, all tests, dev server, and production build

acceptance: go test ./... exits 0 in the scaffolded backend packages.
The frontend dependency manifest includes TypeScript, React, React Flow, and Vite.
The production backend serves index.html for non-API non-static routes.
The development frontend proxy forwards /api/* requests to the Go backend.
One documented command runs backend tests, one runs frontend tests, one runs all tests, one starts local development, and one builds production assets.

depends_on.reason: This is the first implementation task and only consumes the written specification.

constraints: Use Go 1.26.3 or a later Go 1.26.x patch release.
Use TypeScript, React, React Flow, and Vite for the frontend.
Run as a local single-user process with no login or remote account dependency.
non_goals: Multi-user authentication, hosted SaaS deployment, billing, and remote registry publishing.
tasks.notes: Covers FR-001 and FR-005 runtime scaffolding.

tasks.task_name: Create SQLite storage and document directories
tasks.goal: Startup creates the SQLite database, applies DM-001 through DM-008 migrations idempotently, and creates all required local document directories.

inputs.name: runtime-source
inputs.constraints: Backend application entrypoint exists

inputs.name: schema-requirements
inputs.constraints: Includes DM-001 through DM-008 and migration notes

outputs.name: sqlite-schema
outputs.constraints: Tables repositories, repo_sessions, candidates, extraction_plans, modules, blueprints, agent_jobs, settings exist with required constraints

acceptance: Starting with an empty data directory creates data/workbench.sqlite.
Startup creates documents, sessions, modules, blueprints, and jobs directories under the configured data directory.
Running migrations twice leaves the schema unchanged and returns no error.
The agent_jobs_one_active_role_per_subject partial unique index exists.
Unit tests verify every DM-001 through DM-008 table name and required CHECK constraint.
constraints: Use SQLite with WAL mode, foreign keys, and a busy timeout.
SQLite rows store metadata and local filesystem paths for large generated documents.

error_cases.condition: Configured data directory is not writable
error_cases.behavior: Startup returns an error before serving HTTP

tasks.task_name: Define OpenAPI contract and generated types
tasks.goal: openapi.yaml defines every /api/* endpoint, shared schema, error body, and validation constraint required by the workbench API.

inputs.name: api-requirements
inputs.constraints: Includes Surface / API Inventory, API / Schema Contracts, and Error Contract

outputs.name: openapi-spec
outputs.constraints: Contains paths, operation IDs, components, request bodies, response bodies, error responses, enum values, and pagination envelopes

outputs.name: generated-api-types
outputs.constraints: Backend validators and frontend API types are generated from or checked against openapi.yaml

acceptance: openapi.yaml contains operations for every endpoint listed in the Surface / API Inventory.
Every error code in the Error Contract appears in an OpenAPI response component or endpoint response.
OpenAPI schemas reject unknown request fields for write endpoints.
A contract check command exits non-zero when an implemented /api/* route is absent from openapi.yaml.
Frontend API types are generated from or checked against openapi.yaml by the test command.
constraints: openapi.yaml is the source of truth for external API request and response shapes.
Markdown prose must not become the runtime schema source.
tasks.notes: Covers FR-047 and the API / Schema Contracts section.

tasks.task_name: Add runtime config, path safety, and logs
tasks.goal: The backend exposes health and config endpoints, enforces allowed local repository roots, rejects unsafe paths, and writes structured operational logs.

inputs.name: runtime-source
inputs.constraints: Backend server and storage layer exist

inputs.name: openapi-spec
inputs.constraints: Defines /api/health and /api/config responses

outputs.name: config-and-safety-source
outputs.constraints: Implements --data-dir, repeated --allowed-root, CODE_WORKBENCH_ALLOWED_ROOTS, safe relative path resolution, and JSON logs

acceptance: GET /api/config returns analysis limit 4, extraction limit 2, wiring limit 1, configured data directory, allowed roots, and enabled provider names.
When neither allowed-root source is set, local repository roots default to the backend startup working directory.
When the configured allowed-root source is explicitly empty, local path repository registration returns 400 path.invalid.
Path resolution rejects absolute paths, .. segments, and backslash separators before filesystem lookup.
Tests cover escaping symlinks, valid in-tree symlinks, API request logs, job lifecycle logs, and startup reconciliation logs.
constraints: Use lstat and realpath containment checks for source files and artifact paths.
Do not require Postgres, Redis, hosted queues, or cloud object storage.

error_cases.condition: Resolved path escapes the allowed root through a symlink

tasks.notes: Covers FR-004, FR-042, FR-043, FR-046, and health/config surfaces.

tasks.goal: Users can register repositories, create isolated repo sessions, store extraction intent, list sessions, and read safe session source files.

inputs.name: storage-layer
inputs.constraints: repositories and repo_sessions tables exist

inputs.name: openapi-spec
inputs.constraints: Repository and session endpoints are defined

outputs.name: repository-session-source
outputs.constraints: Implements repository registration, session creation, intent persistence, phase state machine, optimistic locking, and file reads

acceptance: POST /api/repositories accepts readable local_path repositories containing .git when the path is inside an allowed root.
POST /api/repositories rejects duplicate source_type and source_uri pairs with 409 repository.duplicate.
POST /api/sessions clones git_url repositories into data/sessions/{sessionId}/repo and removes partial checkout directories on clone failure.
POST /api/sessions/{sessionId}/intent writes the intent JSON document and changes phase to ready_for_analysis without creating an agent_jobs row.
Invalid session phase transitions return 409 session.invalid_transition and leave the row unchanged.
Conflicting expectedUpdatedAt values return 409 state.conflict.
GET /api/sessions/{sessionId}/files returns in-checkout file content and rejects path escapes with 400 path.invalid.
constraints: Repo analysis is scoped to one repository session at a time.
Git URLs must use https://, ssh://, or git@host:owner/repo.git syntax.
Credentials beyond the user's existing local Git configuration are not accepted.

tasks.task_name: Implement agent job scheduler and prompts
tasks.goal: Agent jobs queue idempotently, respect worker limits, render role prompts, persist lifecycle state, and reconcile interrupted work on startup.

inputs.name: storage-layer
inputs.constraints: agent_jobs table and partial unique active-job index exist

inputs.name: session-api
inputs.constraints: Sessions can queue analysis work after intent is recorded

outputs.name: scheduler-source
outputs.constraints: Implements AgentProvider interface, active-job idempotency, role concurrency, timeout handling, prompt rendering, status polling, cancel flow, and startup reconciliation

acceptance: Queueing an already-active role, subjectType, and subjectId returns 200 with the existing job ID and creates no second active row.
Queueing a new job returns 202 and stores a prompt file at data/jobs/{jobId}/prompt.md before the provider starts.
The scheduler runs at most 4 analysis jobs, 2 extraction jobs, and 1 wiring job concurrently.
Jobs that exceed timeout_seconds become failed with error_code job.timeout.
Startup marks orphaned running jobs failed with error_code job.interrupted when the provider cannot prove the tmux process is active.
Unit tests exercise a fake AgentProvider without changing API request shapes or database schema.
constraints: Agent prompts must be rendered with Go text/template from templates/prompts/{role}.md.tmpl.
The provider interface must define Start, Open, Cancel, and Status methods.

error_cases.output: Queued job starts when capacity becomes available

tasks.notes: Covers FR-011, FR-014, FR-015, FR-016, FR-044, and FR-045 scheduler behavior.

tasks.goal: The first AgentProvider launches Claude Code CLI in an isolated tmux session and exposes safe open and cancel controls.

inputs.name: scheduler-source
inputs.constraints: AgentProvider interface and job workspace conventions exist

inputs.name: provider-requirements
inputs.constraints: Includes FR-012 through FR-013 provider command, environment, sandbox, and timeout rules

outputs.name: claude-provider-source
outputs.constraints: Starts Claude Code with required flags, env allowlist, tmux session naming, isolated cwd, denied paths, writable output root, and attach command response

acceptance: A started job persists agent_jobs.tmux_session_name before Open returns an attach command.
The provider invokes Claude Code with --bare, --permission-mode acceptEdits, the required allowedTools list, and disallowed WebFetch and WebSearch tools.
The provider sets HOME to data/jobs/{jobId}/home and only passes PATH, HOME, TMPDIR, SHELL, TERM, and Workbench job variables.
The provider rejects any produced artifact whose realpath is outside the allowed output root before marking a job succeeded.
POST /api/agent-jobs/{jobId}/open returns 409 agent_job.not_running for jobs with no active tmux session.
Provider tests verify command construction, environment filtering, attach command response, and cancel behavior with tmux execution mocked.
constraints: Do not use --add-dir outside the job workspace.
Apply an OS-level filesystem sandbox or equivalent wrapper that denies writes outside the writable output root before the process starts.

tasks.task_name: Implement candidate import and review APIs
tasks.goal: Successful analysis jobs import session-scoped candidates and users can modify, approve, reject, defer, rescan, duplicate, and view candidate source safely.

inputs.name: scheduler-source
inputs.constraints: Analysis job completion can trigger output import

inputs.name: candidate-contract
inputs.constraints: Defines CandidateReport and candidate action schemas

outputs.name: candidate-source
outputs.constraints: Implements CandidateReport import, namespaced candidate IDs, candidate state machine, reason validation, and source file view integration

acceptance: A succeeded repo_analysis job imports data/jobs/{jobId}/candidate-report.json transactionally.
Every imported candidate ID matches {sessionId}.cand.NNN.
Import rejects a candidate missing proposed ports with 422 candidate_report.invalid and persists no partial row for that candidate.
Approve, reject, defer, and duplicate actions require a reason with at least 3 non-whitespace characters.
PATCH /api/candidates/{candidateId} returns 409 candidate.invalid_transition for an approved candidate and leaves the row unchanged.
Candidate source viewing reads through GET /api/sessions/{sessionId}/files and never exposes arbitrary filesystem paths.
constraints: No extraction job may start until the relevant candidate is explicitly approved by the user.
Candidate IDs are namespaced by session ID.

tasks.goal: Extraction plans are created only from approved candidates and extraction jobs operate only on candidates listed in the plan document.

inputs.name: candidate-source
inputs.constraints: Candidate statuses and approval reasons are persisted

inputs.name: scheduler-source
inputs.constraints: Extraction jobs can be queued through the job scheduler

outputs.name: extraction-plan-source
outputs.constraints: Implements extraction plan creation, approved-candidate checks, provenance exclusions, plan document persistence, and extraction job queueing

acceptance: POST /api/extraction-plans with only approved candidate IDs returns 201 and writes a plan document.
POST /api/extraction-plans with any unapproved candidate ID returns 409 candidate.not_approved.
Rejected candidate IDs are stored in the plan only as provenance exclusions.
Extraction prompt rendering includes approved candidate IDs and excludes rejected candidate IDs from work instructions.
POST /api/extraction-plans/{planId}/jobs queues an extraction job that reads the plan document as its source of truth.
constraints: Extraction jobs must not operate on rejected candidate IDs.
Extraction jobs must use the extraction plan document as the source of truth.

tasks.goal: Complete passing extraction outputs register as versioned modules and available modules appear in the workbench palette with duplicate classification support.

inputs.name: extraction-plan-source
inputs.constraints: Extraction job outputs are linked from agent_jobs

inputs.name: module-contract
inputs.constraints: Defines ModuleManifest, registry comparison report, and workbench palette schemas

outputs.name: module-registry-source
outputs.constraints: Implements module registration, semver minor increments, manifest and schema validation, test_status gating, palette listing, and comparison classifications

acceptance: A module is not registered unless source code, tests, manifest, typed ports, configuration schema, provenance metadata, docs, semver version, and test status are present.
The first successful extraction of a candidate registers version 0.1.0.
A later successful re-extraction of the same candidate registers the next minor version and does not replace the existing module row.
available_in_workbench is 1 only when test_status is passing and manifest, ports, and config schema parse successfully.
GET /api/workbench/palette lists only available modules and selects the highest semver version for duplicate module names.
POST /api/modules/{moduleId}/compare returns exactly one classification from new_module, duplicate, variant, adapter_needed, merge_candidate, or reject_duplicate using the spec thresholds.
constraints: Go modules are the default extraction output target unless the approved candidate specifies another target language.
Manifest, ports, and config schema must parse before workbench availability is set.

tasks.task_name: Implement blueprint validation and wiring APIs
tasks.goal: Blueprint APIs store semantic documents separately from flow layouts, validate typed module graphs, and queue wiring jobs only for valid blueprints.

inputs.name: module-registry-source
inputs.constraints: Available modules expose typed ports and palette definitions

inputs.name: scheduler-source
inputs.constraints: Wiring jobs can be queued with role-specific output roots

outputs.name: blueprint-source
outputs.constraints: Implements blueprint create/list/get/update, semantic validation, port compatibility, separate flow layout persistence, and wiring job queueing

acceptance: Saving a blueprint writes a semantic document path and a separate flow layout path.

Validation rejects duplicate node IDs, missing module references, missing required input connections, invalid required config values, invalid target language, invalid output kind, and invalid package name.
Connecting an output port to an input port with a different trimmed type string returns 422 blueprint.port_type_mismatch.
A blueprint with validation_status invalid returns 409 blueprint.invalid when a wiring job is queued.
A blueprint with validation_status valid queues one active wiring job and writes generated artifacts under data/blueprints/{blueprintId}/generated/{jobId} when the job succeeds.
constraints: Port name matching uses ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]{0,63}$.
Port type comparison is case-sensitive exact string equality after trimming ASCII whitespace.
React Flow layout state is never embedded in the semantic blueprint document.

tasks.task_name: Build repositories, sessions, and jobs UI
tasks.goal: The frontend renders repository registration, session lifecycle, intent capture, and agent job monitoring screens using the generated API types.

inputs.name: repository-session-source
inputs.constraints: Repository and session APIs are implemented

inputs.name: scheduler-source
inputs.constraints: Agent job APIs are implemented

outputs.name: repo-session-job-ui
outputs.constraints: Contains Repositories, Sessions, and Agent Jobs screens with typed API client integration

acceptance: The Repositories screen can submit local_path and git_url registration requests and display duplicate repository errors.
The Sessions screen shows repo name, session phase, active job role, timestamps, and next action for each session.
The session detail flow records extraction intent before the analysis queue action becomes available.
The Agent Jobs screen shows queued, running, succeeded, failed, and cancelled jobs with provider, role, subject, tmux session name, timestamps, open action, and cancel action.
Visible running jobs are polled through GET /api/agent-jobs/{jobId} every 5 seconds.
constraints: Use generated or checked frontend API types from openapi.yaml.
No authentication UI is added.
tasks.notes: Covers FR-036, FR-037, and FR-041 for the operational screens.

tasks.task_name: Build candidate review and module registry UI
tasks.goal: The frontend renders candidate review and module registry screens with all required filters, fields, and candidate actions.

inputs.name: candidate-source
inputs.constraints: Candidate APIs are implemented

inputs.name: module-registry-source
inputs.constraints: Module APIs are implemented

outputs.name: candidate-module-ui
outputs.constraints: Contains Candidates and Modules screens with typed API client integration

acceptance: The Candidates screen groups candidates by repo session.
Candidate filters cover session, repo, status, risk, confidence, and capability.
Candidate actions include approve, reject, modify, defer, request rescan, mark duplicate, and view source.
Reason-required candidate actions display the backend error when the reason has fewer than 3 non-whitespace characters.
The Modules screen shows name, version, source repo, source candidate, language, kind, capabilities, ports, test status, docs path, and workbench availability.
The Modules screen can trigger registry comparison and display the returned classification.
constraints: Candidate source viewing must call the session file endpoint rather than using arbitrary filesystem paths.
The UI must not allow extraction plan creation from unapproved candidates.

tasks.task_name: Build React Flow workbench and blueprint UI
tasks.goal: The frontend provides a React Flow workbench that composes registered modules, validates blueprints, saves flow layout separately, and queues wiring jobs.

inputs.name: module-registry-source
inputs.constraints: Workbench palette endpoint lists available modules

inputs.name: blueprint-source
inputs.constraints: Blueprint APIs and wiring job queueing are implemented

outputs.name: workbench-blueprint-ui
outputs.constraints: Contains Workbench and Blueprints screens with React Flow canvas, palette, inspector, validation, save, and generate code controls

acceptance: The Workbench screen lists available modules from GET /api/workbench/palette as draggable node definitions.
Workbench nodes show required and optional typed input ports and typed output ports from module manifests.
Creating an incompatible edge displays the 422 blueprint.port_type_mismatch response and does not persist the edge.
Saving a blueprint sends separate semantic document and flow layout payloads to the backend.
The Workbench screen provides module palette, React Flow canvas, inspector, validation status, save action, validate action, and generate code action.
The Blueprints screen lists saved blueprints and can open validation reports and wiring job status.
constraints: Use React Flow for graph composition.
Do not embed authentication flows or remote account dependencies.
tasks.notes: Covers FR-040 and blueprint UI portions of FR-036.

tasks.goal: The local test suite verifies the complete repository-to-candidate-to-module-to-blueprint-to-wiring smoke path without authentication.

inputs.name: backend-source
inputs.constraints: Backend APIs for sessions, candidates, modules, blueprints, jobs, storage, and safety are implemented

inputs.name: frontend-source
inputs.constraints: All seven primary UI screens are implemented

outputs.name: verification-suite
outputs.constraints: Commands include backend unit tests, frontend tests, integration tests, OpenAPI drift check, and E2E smoke flow
outputs.destination: tests, frontend tests, scripts, CI configuration

acceptance: go test ./... exits 0 under Go 1.26.3 or a later Go 1.26.x patch release.
The frontend unit or component test command exits 0.
A startup test with an empty data directory creates SQLite and all required document directories.
A smoke test registers a repo, creates a session, records intent, queues analysis, imports candidates, approves a candidate, and creates an extraction plan.
A smoke test registers a passing module, opens the Workbench, adds a node, connects compatible ports, saves and validates a blueprint.
A smoke test queues a wiring job for a valid blueprint and displays the returned tmux attach command.
CI or the all-tests command fails when any implemented /api/* route, request schema, response schema, status code, error code, or generated frontend API type drifts from openapi.yaml.
constraints: Tests use fake or isolated provider boundaries where needed instead of requiring real remote services.
The smoke path runs against local filesystem documents and SQLite.
tasks.notes: Covers Testing Requirements and SC-001 through SC-013.
